title: Zwiad Regulatory Monitoring
excerpt: A multi-agent system that automates monitoring of U.S. privacy, cybersecurity, and AI regulations, transforming daily regulatory noise into verified, fact-checked reports routed through human approval into a personal knowledge base.

title: Application of External Standards in AI and Data Privacy Law in the U.S. and E.U.
excerpt: Comprehensive analysis of how legislators in both jurisdictions increasingly delegate technical AI and privacy regulation to external standards from bodies like ISO and NIST, bridging the gap between fast-moving technology and slow legislative cycles.

title: Incentives or Obligations? The U.S. Regulatory Approach to Voluntary Standards
excerpt: Examination of how U.S. states are weaving voluntary AI governance frameworks like NIST's AI Risk Management Framework into statute, transforming industry best practices into de facto legal requirements.

title: FTC Enforcement Actions: A Research Database for Privacy and Data Security Practitioners
excerpt: An interactive database consolidating 285+ FTC enforcement actions in privacy and data security from 1997-2026, featuring searchable provisions, analytics dashboards, and downloadable JSON files for LLM-assisted analysis.

title: Introducing TechRegParser
excerpt: An open-source multi-agent system that reads technology regulation statutes and produces standardized, citation-verified structured extraction in JSON format, solving the problem of statutory formatting inconsistencies across jurisdictions.

title: Stop Processing Orders under the GDPR
excerpt: Research into how common cease-processing orders are among Data Protection Authority enforcement actions, utilizing document analysis scripts and AI tools to examine approximately 2,086 DPA enforcement orders from enforcementtracker.com.

title: Annotated Bibliography: A New AI Agency
excerpt: Comprehensive exploration of academic perspectives on whether a dedicated AI regulatory agency should be established versus empowering existing sectoral regulators, examining arguments for centralized oversight versus distributed approaches.

title: Comments for NIST's "Zero Draft" on Testing, Evaluation, Verification, and Validation for Artificial Intelligence
excerpt: Policy recommendations addressing auditor access levels to AI models and exemptions for open-sourced systems, balancing intellectual property concerns with effective evaluation and accountability requirements for AI systems.
